ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ka mìŋ́ Kɔraawiizi daŋ gil nuukpaŋ la n nii n dɔɔwɛ la chi nɛnnɛ. Kpogli i haaŋ, i biibaala ani ba haana maa zuu a gbera biini.
translated: When Kɔraawi arrived, he saw many people gathered there. He greeted them, and they all responded with joy.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Denii ba pɛɛzuu, “Ka lee i haaŋ Saara duɛi?” Abraham ŋɔ dí u du tanti dia biini.
translated: Then they asked him, “Where is Sarah your wife?” Abraham said to them, “She is in the tent.”

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Denii a baal pɛɛzuu, “I sɔnnɛ ɛrɛɛ?” U ŋɔ, “N sɔnnɛ Jekɔb.”
translated: The man asked, "What is your name?" He replied, "My name is Jacob."

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
text: A saŋa ani saŋa bɛl n wa helle, Nɛfilim faa du durnyaa. A saŋaa Kɔraawiizi biibaala ni newea biihaana faa lul biizi, ba hel kpabaa. Baabuu faa ɛɛ kpeglega di dɔɔ fɔŋŋa nɛr maa zum ba wia, di lii a saŋa di wa hel zinaa.
translated: And when the sons of God saw the daughters of men, that they were fair, they took them wives of all which they chose. And the Lord said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also is flesh: yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: When I go to the market, I will buy many things. When I go to the market, I will buy some food and some clothes.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: This is the new way, it is good. This is the way to bring back our culture, so that our language will live.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: Hello, I am going to the market to buy some things, I will return soon. I will come back home after I finish shopping.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: If you want to be a leader, you must be humble. If you want to be a follower, be diligent and work hard.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: The days of Zilpa were good in the eyes of Jacob.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob said to him, "Korawizi, you are a liar!"

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Denii Kɔraawiizi ŋɔ u be, “Hɛssi anɛɛ hinaa du i hia, a hɛssi anɛɛ naa hina daŋ dɔm. Bɛl daŋ du kpeg kaali u dɔŋ, nabɔma hiŋ danna sɛɛ bichala hiŋ dɔɔra.”
translated: The days of Korawaazi are here, and it is said, “There is no other way to live, there is no other way to die.  Let us live our lives and die with dignity, let us not be ashamed of our ancestors.”

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: Denii u binni Sɔdɔm ni Gɔmɔra barii ani lɛl la maa, di na nyɔɔzi n na lii a hɛri maa guŋ kuull alugɔ kunzeŋ n na fuuh.
translated: The people of Sodom and Gomorrah were wicked and sinful, and they did evil in the sight of the Lord.

ref: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
text: Hɔɔŋ wa pizɛɛ, Joosɛf ba ba be, u na lugɔ ba biine chɔkkɔɔ.
translated: Joseph, the son of Jacob, went to Egypt.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Kɔ́raawiizi ɔɔ tɛi sɔnii di gil i pattikuna lɛɛ, í na mia ni siŋ werge.
translated: The girl went to the market to buy things, she found a nice cloth.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: U kaa lul horohiŋ, u dau u ge sɔŋ Sɛla. A Sɛla lulaa saŋa, di ba jaa du bol la banna yir Kɛzib.
translated: When the sun rises, he will go to greet Sela. When Sela sees him, he will give him a gift of a bull from his herd.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: I am going to the market. I am going to buy some food, so that I can eat.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: Today, I will go to the market to buy some things, I will go there in the morning. I will also buy some food for my family.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: When I was young, I was very playful. Now that I am old, I am very quiet.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Denii Zilpa gave birth to a son named Jacob.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob said to him, "Korawizi, you are a great man!" He then went and sat down by Mahanam.
